question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a PartialBlockIdentifier with only a Hash field (no Index) to RetrieveBlock() causing the system to resolve a different block than intended, thereby presenting a reorganized view of transaction history at an attacker-chosen consensus timestamp, without any authentication check?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a PartialBlockIdentifier with only an Index field (no Hash) to RetrieveBlock() to anchor the balance query to an arbitrary historical block's ConsensusEndNanos, causing the reported transaction history to reflect a reorganized state at that timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a PartialBlockIdentifier with both Hash and Index fields where the hash belongs to a different block than the index, causing FindByIdentifier() to resolve an unexpected block and present a reorganized balance history to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user omit the BlockIdentifier entirely in an AccountBalanceRequest, forcing RetrieveLatest() to return the second-latest block (by design), and exploit this off-by-one block selection to present a transaction history that excludes the most recent confirmed block's transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an AccountBalanceRequest with a BlockIdentifier pointing to the genesis block, causing RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() to compute balances at the earliest possible ConsensusEndNanos and present a transaction history that omits all subsequent transfers, effectively reorganizing history to the chain's origin?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hex-encoded alias address to NewAccountIdFromString() that resolves to a different entity at different historical block timestamps, causing AccountBalance() to return balances tied to a reorganized account identity across different block queries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user query AccountBalance() with a deleted account's address at a block timestamp after deletion, exploiting the balanceChangeEndTimestamp/balanceSnapshotEndTimestamp split in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() to present a transaction history that misrepresents the account's final state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user query AccountBalance() with a block index of 0 (genesis) to anchor RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() at ConsensusEndNanos of the genesis block, causing the system to present a transaction history that shows no transfers have occurred, reorganizing the visible history to an empty state?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a PartialBlockIdentifier with a very large Index value to FindByIndex() causing it to resolve a future or non-existent block, and if the error path is inconsistent, present a reorganized or empty transaction history to the caller?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that RetrieveLatest() intentionally returns the second-latest block to repeatedly query AccountBalance() without a BlockIdentifier, causing integrators to see a consistently stale transaction history that excludes the latest confirmed block's transfers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AllAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user call AllAccountBalances() with a BlockIdentifier pointing to an arbitrary historical block, causing the delegation to AccountBalance() to compute balances at that block's ConsensusEndNanos and present a reorganized transaction history without any additional authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AllAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AllAccountBalances() directly delegates to AccountBalance() without any additional validation, bypassing any future rate-limiting or access controls that might be added to AccountBalance() but not to AllAccountBalances()?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block hash with a '0x' prefix to RetrieveBlock(), which calls SafeRemoveHexPrefix() before passing to FindByHash(), and craft a hash that after prefix stripping matches a different block than intended, causing a reorganized history view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a block hash with multiple '0x' prefixes or malformed hex encoding to RetrieveBlock(), causing SafeRemoveHexPrefix() to produce an unexpected hash string that resolves to a different block, reorganizing the transaction history anchor point?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an account address in 'shard.realm.num' format with a shard/realm that differs from systemShard/systemRealm to NewAccountIdFromString(), causing the balance query to resolve to a different entity's transaction history than the one the caller intended?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user query AccountBalance() at a block timestamp that falls exactly on a balance snapshot boundary, exploiting the snapshot/balance-change calculation in RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() to present a transaction history that either includes or excludes transfers at that exact nanosecond depending on boundary condition handling?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user query AccountBalance() at a block whose ConsensusEndNanos is exactly equal to an account's deletion timestamp, exploiting the off-by-one in balanceSnapshotEndTimestamp = balanceChangeEndTimestamp - 1 to present a transaction history that misrepresents the account's balance at deletion time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly query AccountBalance() with different historical block identifiers to enumerate the full transaction history of any account, effectively reconstructing a reorganized timeline of all transfers without any access control preventing historical data enumeration?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a PartialBlockIdentifier with a negative Index value to RetrieveBlock(), and if FindByIndex() does not reject it before passing to the database layer, cause an unexpected block resolution that presents a reorganized history view?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that AccountBalance() uses block.ConsensusEndNanos as the timestamp for RetrieveBalanceAtBlock(), and by supplying a block identifier for a block with an unusually large ConsensusEndNanos, cause the balance calculation to include transfers from a future snapshot period, reorganizing the visible history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user query AccountBalance() for an account that has been re-created after deletion (same alias, different entity), supplying a historical block identifier, to cause RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() to return the balance of the old entity rather than the new one, presenting a reorganized identity-to-history mapping?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hex alias address to AccountBalance() that resolves to an account which has been deleted and re-created, causing getCryptoEntity() to return the first match (current alias owner) even at historical block timestamps where the old entity owned the alias, reorganizing the account-to-history association?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the metadata field in AccountBalanceResponse, where account_id is only populated when accountId.HasAlias() and accountIdString != '', to infer whether an alias resolves to a different entity at a given historical block, enabling reconstruction of account reorganization history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEd25519PublicKey()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a hex alias address whose decoded bytes satisfy the isEd25519PublicKey() check (len==34, bytes[0]==0x12, bytes[1]==0x20) but do not correspond to a valid Ed25519 key, causing the public_key metadata to be populated with attacker-controlled bytes that mislead downstream consumers about the account's signing history?
